title: RUN with bind mount incorrectly resolves symlinks
body:
### Issue Description

When using `RUN --mount=type=bind,source=/dist/,target=/opt/dist/ true`, I'm getting an error if source points to symlink.

`/dist` points to `/full/path/to/linked/directory/except/root` and located at `/working/dir/where/containerfile/is/dist`.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Add symlink (e.g. `ln -s $(mktemp) ./dist`)
2. Add `RUN --mount=type=bind,source=/dist/,target=/opt/dist/ true`
3. Run `podman build`


### Describe the results you received

```
Error: building at STEP "RUN --mount=type=bind,source=/dist.l/,target=/opt/dist/ true": resolving mountpoints for container "4f2d0a535c28badaf3b81d4e6f2e5d975063d3678e383fb2666e3c3fb298c57c": sanitizing bind subdirectory "/working/dir/where/containerfile/is/full/path/to/linked/directory/except/root": opening directory "full/path/to/linked/directory/except/root" under "/working/dir/where/containerfile/is": no such file or directory: no such file or directory
```

### Describe the results you expected

No error (if symlink points to existing location).

comments.body: I imagine this is a result of a CVE fix. @nalind can confirm but he's off for the week.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
